prompts.text: Give us three reasons people procrastinate on things they care about.
Tell a younger sibling three things you wish you'd learned earlier in life.
Name three habits worth building in your twenties.
Pitch a friend on your hometown using three reasons to visit.
Give three reasons it's worth saying sorry first, even when you're not fully wrong.
Tell us three things that make a friendship last.
Convince a roommate to split groceries with you using three points.
Give your manager three reasons you should take Friday off.
Name three signs a relationship has run its course.
Tell a stranger three things they should know before moving to your city.
Give three reasons writing things by hand still matters.
Convince a skeptical friend to try therapy using three points.
Name three things people get wrong about confidence.
Tell your parents three reasons you're taking a year off from your plan.
Give three reasons it's okay to outgrow old friendships.
Name three things worth spending more money on than most people do.
Argue in three points that boredom is good for you.
In three points, tell a wedding crowd what makes the couple work.
Give three reasons most people misunderstand what success actually feels like.
Persuade a city council in three points to keep the local library open.
Tell us about a time you changed your mind about something important.
Walk us through the first time you stood up for yourself.
Tell the story of a friendship that surprised you.
Describe the day you realized you needed to leave a job, relationship, or place.
Tell us about a small moment from childhood that shaped how you think today.
Walk us through a time you tried something new and embarrassed yourself.
Tell the story of a trip that didn't go the way you planned.
Describe a conversation that changed your relationship with a family member.
Tell us about the time you almost gave up on something but didn't.
Walk us through the night you finally said the thing you'd been holding back.
Tell the story of someone you misjudged and how you found out.
Describe a moment you got exactly what you wanted and how it actually felt.
Tell us about a time a stranger helped you in a way you'll never forget.
Walk us through the day you lost something that mattered.
Tell the story of a habit you broke and how the breaking actually happened.
Describe a time you trusted your gut over the advice everyone was giving you.
Tell us about a moment you realized your parents were just people.
Walk us through the slowest fight you've ever been in.
Tell the story of a goal you achieved and then immediately questioned.
Describe a forgiveness that took you years to give.
Should you take the job that pays more or the one you'd enjoy more?
Is it better to rent or buy in your twenties?
Tell a friend whether they should text their ex back.
Should you go to the wedding of someone you barely talk to anymore?

Should you tell your boss you're unhappy at work?

Should parents read their teenager's messages?
Is it better to live near family or far from them?
Tell your group whether you should pool money for a big shared gift.

Is it ever okay to skip a funeral?
Should you keep a friendship that only exists online?

Is it better to plan a wedding small or go all in?
Should you go to the reunion you've been dreading?
Tell a friend whether they should move in with their partner.
Is it worth telling someone a secret that will hurt them?
Should you stay in a city you love for a job you hate?
Tell your family whether you should keep hosting the holidays this year.
What's the biggest source of friction in your morning routine and how would you fix it?
Name something that's broken about how friends stay in touch and pitch a fix.
Tell us a problem with how people use their phones at dinner and how to solve it.
What's wrong with how most people pack for trips and what should they do instead?
Identify a problem with how chores get split in shared homes and pitch a fix.
Name what's broken about small talk and propose a better version.
What's the biggest issue with how people give compliments and how would you change it?
Identify a problem with how people apologize and pitch a real solution.
Tell us what's broken about how groups pick a restaurant and how you'd fix it.
Name a problem with how families handle holidays and pitch a solution.
What's wrong with how most people give advice and what should they do instead?
Identify the pain point in long-distance friendships and propose a fix.
Tell us what's broken about wedding planning and pitch a solution.
Name a problem with how people handle disappointment and how to address it.
What's wrong with how most people end conversations and what's the fix?
Identify a problem with how new neighbors meet each other and pitch a solution.
Tell us what's broken about how people set New Year's resolutions and propose a better way.
Name a problem with how couples handle money disagreements and pitch a fix.

Convince your roommates to start a weekly shared dinner night.
Persuade your family to take a phone-free vacation this year.
Move your friends to commit to a monthly hangout that actually happens.

Persuade your partner to plan one surprise for each other every month.
Move your team at work to schedule walking meetings.

Persuade a friend who's been isolating to come out this weekend.
Move your parents to finally take that trip they've been postponing for years.
Convince your group of friends to do a no-gifts holiday season.
Persuade a younger relative to take a gap year before college.
Move a stubborn family member to go to a doctor's appointment they've been avoiding.
Convince your community to support a new local park.

Move a friend stuck in a bad relationship to take the first step out.

Explain to someone new in town what they need to know to feel local.

Walk us through how you'd plan a big birthday for someone you love.
Explain to a friend what they should know before quitting their job.
Tell a first-time host what to expect from running a dinner party.
Walk a friend through what they should think about before getting married.
Explain to your past self what to expect from your thirties.
Tell a roomful of high schoolers what to expect from adulthood, structured up front.
Walk an immigrant friend through what to expect from their first year here.
Explain to a couple thinking about divorce what to expect from the process emotionally.
Tell us two everyday joys, then the one that means the most.
Name two small habits, then the one that actually changed your life.
Give two reasons to travel, then the one most people miss.
Tell us two things you love about your best friend, then the one that defines them.
Name two reasons to keep a journal, then the one that surprised you.
Give two reasons to call your parents this week, then the one that matters most.
Tell us two things that make a good apology, then the one most people skip.
Name two reasons to leave a job, then the one that finally pushes you out.
Give two reasons people fear change, then the one underneath all of them.
Tell us two things you'd tell your younger self, then the one that would have actually landed.
Name two reasons long friendships last, then the one nobody talks about.
Give two things you're proud of, then the one you almost didn't mention.
Tell us two ways you've grown this year, then the one that surprised you most.
Name two reasons people stay in bad situations, then the deepest one.
Give two reasons silence is powerful in a conversation, then the one that hits hardest.
Tell us two compliments you've received, then the one you still think about.
Name two reasons people regret choices, then the regret most people share.
Give two reasons forgiveness is hard, then the real reason underneath.
Tell us two truths about getting older, then the one nobody warns you about.
Name two reasons people stay quiet when they should speak up, then the one that costs the most.
Compare growing up in a small town vs a big city.
Contrast spending a Saturday alone vs spending it with friends.
Compare being a morning person vs a night person.
Contrast traveling solo vs traveling with someone you love.
Compare reading a book vs watching the movie version.
Contrast living with roommates vs living alone.
Compare how friendship feels at twenty vs at forty.
Contrast giving advice vs actually listening.
Compare being the oldest sibling vs being the youngest.
Contrast a job that pays well vs a job you love.
Compare the version of yourself you show online vs the one your family sees.
Contrast confidence vs arrogance.
Compare being in love at twenty-two vs being in love at thirty-five.
Contrast keeping a promise vs breaking one for the right reason.
Compare what your parents wanted for you vs what you actually want.
Contrast forgiving someone vs forgetting what they did.
Compare ambition that comes from joy vs ambition that comes from fear.
Contrast the way grief looks at the start vs how it looks a year in.
Compare freedom you choose vs freedom that's handed to you.
Contrast loving someone the way they want vs loving them the way you know how.
How do you know when it's time to walk away from something?
What do you do when a friend goes silent on you?

Why do small choices end up mattering more than big ones?
How do you tell someone you love that they're wrong?
What do you do when you realize you've outgrown a friendship?

How do you recover from a moment of public embarrassment?
What do you do when your gut and your plan disagree?
Why does nervousness sometimes feel exactly like excitement?
How do you tell the difference between giving up and letting go?
What's the best way to handle a friend who's clearly jealous of you?

Why does saying goodbye get harder as you get older?

Why is the truth almost never the thing we're afraid of?
